Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 3, under '3. Results', it states: 'The GETUG-AFU15 trial randomized 385 patients including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The ADT arm is the control arm.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The median follow-up duration is explicitly stated in the 'Results and limitations' section of the abstract on page 1: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo, median OS in the overall population was 62.1 mo...' and also in Section 3.1 'Overall survival' on page 3: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9-84.7)...'. Additionally, Table 2 on page 4 lists 'Median follow-up, mo' as '83.9' for GETUG-AFU 15.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel; Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Alone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The title of the paper states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section in the abstract further confirms this: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'.

Treatment Arm - N.value: 192 in the ADT plus D arm
Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The experimental arm is 'ADT plus D'.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract, it states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. On page 2, in the 'Patients and methods' section, it further confirms: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' This value is also repeated on page 3 in the 'Results' section.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The abstract states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". This indicates two treatment arms: ADT plus D and ADT alone. This is further confirmed in the 'Patients and methods' section on page 2: "385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D... or ADT alone.".

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' This describes the specific regimen for Treatment Arm 1.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Hormonal therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy) and Chemotherapy (Docetaxel)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The title of the paper states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1 and '2. Patients and methods' section on page 2 confirm that patients were randomized to 'ADT plus D' or 'ADT alone'. ADT is described as 'orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors', which are hormonal therapies. Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Control Arm.evidence: The title of the paper states "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone". The 'ADT Alone' arm serves as the comparison group. In the 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1, it mentions "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states "385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." Table 1 on page 3 also lists "ADT (n = 193)" as one of the arms, which is the control arm.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival, progression-free survival, and metastatic burden. There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale' or any assessment of participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status in the context of the GETUG-AFU15 trial. Reference [26] mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12), but this is not a finding from the current study.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS' and 'Median Progression-Free Survival' for the treatment arm (ADT plus D), but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for this arm.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS' and 'Median Progression-Free Survival' for the control arm (ADT alone), but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for this arm. It mentions 'salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo' in the ADT arm, but this refers to subsequent treatment, not the initial on-treatment duration for the control therapy itself.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document describes the GETUG-AFU15 trial and its outcomes, focusing on overall survival, biochemical progression-free survival, and radiographic progression-free survival. There is no mention or reporting of results related to the quality of life of participants in the main text, abstract, or results sections. While a reference (number 26) mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12), it does not indicate that QoL was reported for the GETUG-AFU15 trial discussed in this paper.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Age, yr, median (IQR)', under the column 'ADT plus D (n = 192)' on page 3.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Age, yr, median (IQR)', under the column 'ADT (n = 193)' on page 3.

NCT.evidence: Found on page 3, under '3. Results', stating 'The trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00104715).'

Trial Name.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial
Trial Name.evidence: Extracted from the main title of the article on page 1.

Author.evidence: Extracted the first author 'Gwenaelle Gravis' and used 'et al.' for the rest of the author list from the top of page 1.

Year.evidence: Found in the journal header on page 1: 'EUROPEAN UROLOGY 70 (2016)256-262'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page journal article with detailed sections (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, References), indicating it is a full publication, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title of the article on page 1: 'Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial'. Also confirmed in the Abstract: 'Randomized phase 3 trial'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title includes 'Long-term Survival Analysis' and the abstract states 'To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study', indicating it is a follow-up analysis of a previously reported trial.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title of the article: 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial'. It is also consistently referred to as 'GETUG-AFU15 study' or 'GETUG-AFU15 trial' throughout the document, for example, in the Abstract under 'Objective' and 'Design, setting, and participants', and in the 'Patients and methods' section on page 2.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'High-volume disease (%)', column 'ADT plus D (n = 192)' on page 3.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'High-volume disease (%)', column 'ADT (n = 193)' on page 3.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Low-volume disease (%)', column 'ADT (n = 193)' on page 3.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states: '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' This indicates that all 192 patients in the ADT plus D (treatment) arm received docetaxel as part of their initial treatment.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states that 193 patients were in the ADT arm (control arm) and received ADT alone as initial treatment. On page 4, under '4. Discussion', it is mentioned: 'In the ADT arm, 127 of 149 patients (85%) treated for progressive disease received D'. This means 127 out of 193 patients in the control arm received docetaxel as a subsequent treatment during the trial. The percentage is calculated as (127 / 193) * 100 = 65.8%.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS)
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Abstract' section, under 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis', it states: 'Primary end point was OS. Secondary end points were biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS).'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document reports results for 'patients with metastases at diagnosis' on page 2, which refers to synchronous metastases. For example, 'For upfront metastatic patients, OS was 52.6 mo (95% CI, 43.3-66.8) and 41.5 mo (95% CI, 36.3-54.5), respectively (HR: 0.93 [95% CI, 0.69-1.25]; p = 0.6).' Also, on page 5, it mentions 'patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases', where 'de novo' refers to synchronous.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document reports results for 'patients with metastases after failure of local treatment' on page 2, which refers to metachronous diseases. For example, 'For patients with metastases after failure of local treatment, median OS was not reached (95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and was 69.8 mo (95% CI, 62.2-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.83 [95% CI, 0.47-1.47]; p = 0.5) (Supplementary Table 2).' Also, on page 5, it mentions 'patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases', where 'secondary metastatic diseases' refers to metachronous.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The Abstract on page 1 states the objective is 'To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.' On page 2, it explicitly states, 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' HVD is defined as 'high-volume disease'. Results for HVD are presented, for example, on page 3: 'In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm, but the difference was not statistically significant: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0-53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9-43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14) (Fig. 2).'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The Abstract on page 1 states the objective is 'To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.' On page 2, it explicitly states, 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' LVD is defined as 'low-volume disease'. Results for LVD are presented, for example, on page 3: 'In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) in the ADT plus D arm and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67–1.55]; p = 0.9) (Fig. 3).'

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: On page 3, under section '3.1. Overall survival', the text states: '242 patients had died (127 in the ADT arm and 115 in the ADT plus D arm...'. The 'ADT plus D arm' is the treatment arm.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, in section 3.2 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: 'The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo'. bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is defined based on PSA progression, making it equivalent to TTPSA. The ADT plus D arm is the treatment arm.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: On page 4, in section 3.2 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: '...versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7)'. This value corresponds to the ADT arm, which is the control arm. bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is defined based on PSA progression, making it equivalent to TTPSA.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' on page 4. Deaths are Grade 5 events, which are a subset of Grade 3 or higher. However, it only provides the count for Grade 5 treatment-related events, not for all Grade 3 or higher treatment-related events.

The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' (treatment arm) on page 4. There is no information provided for treatment-related adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the control arm (ADT alone).

On page 4, under section 3.2, it states: 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm'. Deaths are considered Grade 5 adverse events. Table 1 on page 3 indicates that N=192 patients were in the ADT plus D (treatment) arm. Therefore, 4/192 = 2.08%.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) versus ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors. Docetaxel was administered at 75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The title of the paper is 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. The 'Patients and methods' section further elaborates: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.' These details describe the type of treatment provided.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 1: 'median OS in the overall population was 62.1 mo (95% confidence interval [CI], 49.5–73.7) ... for ADT plus D'. Confirmed in Table 2, 'GETUG-AFU 15', 'All patients' row, 'ADT plus D, mo' column.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 1: 'median OS in the overall population was ... and 48.6 mo (95% CI, 40.9–60.6) for ... ADT arms, respectively'. Confirmed in Table 2, 'GETUG-AFU 15', 'All patients' row, 'ADT, mo' column.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 1: 'for low-volume disease (LVD) patients; median was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR)'. Confirmed in Table 2, 'GETUG-AFU 15', 'LVD' row, 'ADT plus D, mo' column.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Results and limitations' section on page 1: 'for low-volume disease (LVD) patients; median was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR)'. Confirmed in Table 2, 'GETUG-AFU 15', 'LVD' row, 'ADT, mo' column.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report of a single randomized controlled trial (RCT) and discusses its own results, limitations (e.g., lack of statistical power, retrospective analysis), and comparisons with other RCTs. However, it does not provide an explicit 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' in a standardized format or framework (like GRADE) across multiple RCTs or even for its own evidence in a summarized, extractable form as requested by the label.

Treatment Class.evidence: The clinical trial compares two treatment arms: 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone' (Page 1, Title and Abstract). Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) is a form of hormone therapy, and Docetaxel is a chemotherapy drug. Therefore, the treatments received by participants fall under the classes of Hormone Therapy and Chemotherapy. This classification is further supported by references to similar trials (e.g., CHAARTED trial, cited on page 5), which are described as comparing 'chemohormonal therapy versus hormonal therapy' (References [14] and [15]).
